nl_query: Which scripts have been through more than one evaluation cycle and have at least one result history correction, showing the script URN, number of cycles, number of corrections, and the latest final marks.
failed_sql:
SELECT DISTINCT s.urn, COUNT(DISTINCT ea.evaluation_cycle) AS num_cycles, COUNT(DISTINCT rh.id) AS num_corrections, MAX(r.final_marks) AS latest_final_marks
FROM   answer_script s
JOIN   script_assignment sa ON sa.script_id = s.id AND sa.is_active = TRUE
JOIN   evaluation_attempt ea ON ea.assignment_id = sa.id AND ea.is_active = TRUE
JOIN   result r ON r.script_id = s.id
JOIN   result_history rh ON rh.result_id = r.id
WHERE  EXISTS (
  SELECT 1
  FROM   script_assignment sa2
  WHERE  sa2.script_id = s.id
  AND    sa2.evaluation_cycle > ea.evaluation_cycle
)
GROUP BY s.urn, latest_final_marks
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT rh.id) > 0;
error: Validation failed (schema): Column 'sa2.evaluation_cycle' does not exist. The Postgres planner suggests 'ea.evaluation_cycle' — use that, or verify the column belongs to the qualifying table in your FROM/JOIN clauses.
